perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Recipient agrees, prior to the termination of this Agreement, to provide the Provider with all work products incorporating or derived therefrom or written confirmation of their destruction. [ALTERNATE CLAUSE : Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Upon the written request of the Provider, Recipient shall promptly return to the Provider any and all Confidential Information without retaining any copies or excerpt thereof except the Recipient may keep one (1) copy for evidentiary purposes only. ]
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. **However, Recipient may use the Confidential Information for internal training purposes**. Recipient agrees, prior to the termination of this Agreement, to provide the Provider with all work products incorporating or derived therefrom or written confirmation of their destruction. [ALTERNATE CLAUSE : Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Upon the written request of the Provider, Recipient shall promptly return to the Provider any and all Confidential Information without retaining any copies or excerpt thereof except the Recipient may keep one (1) copy for evidentiary purposes only. ]
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'However, Recipient may use the Confidential Information for internal training purposes', the original restriction on use becomes ambiguous. This **in-text contradiction** allows for a use case outside of 'the Purpose', directly conflicting with the initial statement. This introduces uncertainty as it is not defined, whether this use still is within the bounds of Confidentiality or not, which causes an ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. However, Recipient may use the Confidential Information for internal training purposes. Recipient agrees, prior to the termination of this Agreement, to provide the Provider with all work products incorporating or derived therefrom or written confirmation of their destruction. [ALTERNATE CLAUSE : Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Upon the written request of the Provider, Recipient shall promptly return to the Provider any and all Confidential Information without retaining any copies or excerpt thereof except the Recipient may keep one (1) copy for evidentiary purposes only. ]
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'However, Recipient may use the Confidential Information for internal training purposes' to a clause that initially restricts the use of Confidential Information to only 'in connection with the Purpose'. This addition introduces a use case outside of the 'Purpose', creating a direct conflict and ambiguity within the clause. This is an in-text contradiction, as it allows for a use that was originally prohibited, creating uncertainty and potential enforceability issues. The changed and contradicted text are in the same location, but the change clearly makes the original intention contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disc lose the Confidential Information only to such of its employees [OPTIONAL: and Representatives] who have a need to know such information for the Purpose and according to the terms and conditions contained herein. Recipient shall ensure that its employ ees [OPTIONAL: and Representatives] are fully aware of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the obligations of confidentiality owed to the Provider. Recipient agrees to protect the Provider’s Confidential Information using at le ast the same degree of care Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disc lose the Confidential Information only to such of its employees [OPTIONAL: and Representatives] who have a need to know such information for the Purpose and according to the terms and conditions contained herein. **However, Recipient is not responsible for unauthorized disclosures by its employees and Representatives, provided they have undergone a training program**. Recipient shall ensure that its employ ees [OPTIONAL: and Representatives] are fully aware of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the obligations of confidentiality owed to the Provider. Recipient agrees to protect the Provider’s Confidential Information using at le ast the same degree of care Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, Recipient is not responsible for unauthorized disclosures by its employees and Representatives, provided they have undergone a training program' creates an **in-text contradiction** with the earlier statement that the Recipient 'shall ensure that its employees... are fully aware of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the obligations of confidentiality'. The training program may be insufficient to guarantee prevention of breaches of confidentiality, creating uncertainty about the enforcement. Now the company is only responsible for holding a training and the initial statement becomes non-binding
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient may disc lose the Confidential Information only to such of its employees [OPTIONAL: and Representatives] who have a need to know such information for the Purpose and according to the terms and conditions contained herein. However, Recipient is not responsible for unauthorized disclosures by its employees and Representatives, provided they have undergone a training program. Recipient shall ensure that its employ ees [OPTIONAL: and Representatives] are fully aware of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the obligations of confidentiality owed to the Provider. Recipient agrees to protect the Provider’s Confidential Information using at le ast the same degree of care Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that the Recipient 'shall ensure that its employees... are fully aware of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the obligations of confidentiality', which implies a responsibility for their actions. The changed text adds that 'Recipient is not responsible for unauthorized disclosures by its employees and Representatives, provided they have undergone a training program'. This directly conflicts with the obligation to ensure awareness and confidentiality obligations, as the training program might not prevent all breaches. The contradiction arises because the recipient is both responsible for ensuring awareness and not responsible for unauthorized disclosures after training. The Contradicted text is the same as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*), unless terminated earlier by either Party. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party. The rights and obligations of the Parties under paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement shal l continue beyond termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*), unless terminated earlier by either Party. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party. **However, Provider may only terminate this agreement if there is a breach of the mentioned Confidential Information**. The rights and obligations of the Parties under paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement shal l continue beyond termination.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'However, Provider may only terminate this agreement if there is a breach of the mentioned Confidential Information', a restriction on the Provider's ability to terminate is introduced, creating an **in-text contradiction** with the earlier statement that 'Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason'. Now only the Recipient can terminate for any reason, and the provider can only do so if there is a breach. This causes an ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*), unless terminated earlier by either Party. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party. However, Provider may only terminate this agreement if there is a breach of the mentioned Confidential Information. The rights and obligations of the Parties under paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement shal l continue beyond termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason', granting both parties equal termination rights. The changed text introduces a restriction on the Provider's termination rights, stating 'Provider may only terminate this agreement if there is a breach of the mentioned Confidential Information.' This creates a direct contradiction as the Provider is no longer able to terminate for 'any reason' as the Recipient can, but only for a specific reason. This contradicts the initial broad statement and therefore there is a direct conflict between the two statements, causing uncertainty about the termination clauses and ambiguity in enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information is disclo sed by the Parties without any express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or the completeness thereof. The Parties explicitly disclaim any liability relating to the information, errors or omissions thereform.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information is disclo sed by the Parties without any express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or the completeness thereof. **However, Recipient acknowledges that it is solely responsible to verify the Confidential Information accuracy before using it**. The Parties explicitly disclaim any liability relating to the information, errors or omissions thereform.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'However, Recipient acknowledges that it is solely responsible to verify the Confidential Information accuracy before using it', there is a shift in responsibilities, creating an **in-text contradiction** with the initial statement that 'The Parties explicitly disclaim any liability relating to the information, errors or omissions thereform.'. Even though both parties disclaim any warranty, the recipient has the burden of verifying its veracity, which can be a conflict of interest, and can cause an ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information is disclo sed by the Parties without any express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or the completeness thereof. However, Recipient acknowledges that it is solely responsible to verify the Confidential Information accuracy before using it. The Parties explicitly disclaim any liability relating to the information, errors or omissions thereform.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. Initially, both parties disclaim liability for the accuracy of confidential information. However, the changed text adds a clause placing the sole responsibility on the recipient to verify the accuracy, which contradicts the disclaimer of liability. This creates a direct conflict regarding responsibility and liability for errors or omissions in the confidential information, which aligns with the provided explanation and meets the criteria for a strong in-text contradiction. This is further supported by the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
